content: I'm working on a new listing for a historic property in downtown Austin. The owners want to highlight the 1920s architecture but also appeal to young professionals.
That sounds like an interesting challenge. What specific features does the property have?
Original hardwood floors, crown molding, a clawfoot tub, and those beautiful built-in cabinets. I'm thinking of doing a photoshoot this weekend.
Those features sound perfect for marketing materials. Are you handling the staging yourself or working with a professional?
I usually do my own staging. Between that and taking care of my parents in Denver, I'm pretty busy. My mom's been having some health issues lately, so I try to visit every couple of months.
That must be challenging, balancing work and family responsibilities across states. How long have you been doing that?
About a year now. My dad's retired, so he's there full-time, but I help coordinate their medical appointments and such. It's tough, but family comes first.
I can imagine. Do you have any siblings who help out or is it mostly you?
Just me. I'm an only child. But I've gotten pretty good at managing things remotely. I even set up a smart home system for them so I can check in on their place when I can't be there.
That's really thoughtful of you. What kind of smart home features did you install?

Basic stuff - smart locks, cameras, and a system that monitors for falls. I'm actually thinking of writing a blog post about it for my real estate website. A lot of my clients are retired folks looking to age in place.
That's a great idea! You could position yourself as an expert in that niche market. What else are you working on these days?

Besides the historic property, I've got a modern condo listing downtown. The owner wants to highlight the smart home features - automated lighting, climate control, the works. Funny how my personal interests are becoming professional assets.
It sounds like your hobbies in home automation and technology are really complementing your real estate work. Do you find that helps you connect with certain types of clients?

Absolutely. Especially with the younger buyers who are tech-savvy. I can speak their language when it comes to smart home features. Last week I closed a deal with a couple who were really impressed that I knew the difference between Z-Wave and Zigbee protocols.
That's impressive! It sounds like you've found a unique niche. How do you balance all these different aspects of your work and personal life?

Honestly? Not very well sometimes. I'm usually working on my laptop at 10pm after I've helped my parents with their telemedicine appointments. But I love what I do, and I'm passionate about helping people find their perfect home, whether that's a tech-forward condo or a historic gem with character.

Your dedication is really admirable. It sounds like you've built a meaningful career that aligns with your personal interests and values. Do you have any time for your other hobbies, like cooking or painting?

I try to make time on weekends. I've been experimenting with Thai recipes - my green curry is getting pretty good if I do say so myself. And I paint when I can, though it's been a while. Maybe I should start sketching the properties I'm selling - could be a nice personal touch for clients.
